content.problemStatement:
Create a program to model a hierarchy of shapes.\
You will have a base class called `Shape` and two derived classes, `Square` and `Rectangle`. \
Each shape should have the following properties:

`Shape`
- A pure virtual function `calculateArea()` to calculate the area of the shape.

`Square` (Derived from `Shape`):
- Additional property: `sideLength` (an integer).
- Implementation of `calculateArea()` to calculate the area of the square using the formula: sideLength * sideLength.

`Rectangle` (Derived from `Shape`):
- Additional properties: length and width (both integers).
- Implementation of `calculateArea()` to calculate the area of the rectangle using the formula: length * width.

Given the dimension of square and rectangle. \
Display the area using `calculateArea()`.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- First line contain one integer representing side of square.
- Second line contain two integers representing length and breadth of rectangle respectively.
content.outputFormat: Print the area of square on first line and area of rectangle on second line.
